summary.intent: Address a fourth round of Codex review findings on PR #669 for the ars-ui combobox component, fixing behavioral gaps in keyboard navigation, disabled state handling, prop synchronization, and ARIA attributes.
summary.outcome: All four Codex-identified issues were fixed with regression tests added, CI infrastructure failure was diagnosed and rerun successfully, and the fix was committed and pushed. The snapshot and spec were updated to match the new aria-labelledby behavior.

repo: The combobox machine lives in crates/ars-components/src/selection/combobox/mod.rs and has distinct branches for single vs multi-select modes that must be kept consistent.
Snapshot tests use cargo-insta; `cargo insta accept` or `cargo insta accept -p ars-components` accepts pending snapshots.
The project uses `cargo xci --profile fast` as the pre-commit validation gate (runs fmt, check, clippy, tests, snapshots, spec validation in sequence).
Spec conformance tests live in spec/components/selection/combobox.md and are validated via `cargo xtask spec validate`.
Codex review state is tracked via GitHub reactions (eyes = reviewing, rocket = approved) and PR review thread resolution state via GraphQL API.
The `snapshot-reviewed` label must be re-applied after any snapshot change is pushed.
SyncProps must handle the case where a combobox is disabled mid-open: it should close the popup and fire the close callback.
content_attrs (listbox element) should not emit aria-labelledby when no label element is rendered — aria-label fallback is the correct pattern.

code.finding: ArrowDown on a closed combobox was not highlighting the first item correctly; fix required adjusting the initial highlight computation in the open_plan branch.
Focus event could open the popup even when the combobox was disabled; needed a disabled guard before the open transition.
SyncProps did not handle the disabled=true case when the popup was open; needed to close and notify in that branch.
content_attrs emitted aria-labelledby referencing a label id even when no label was rendered; fixed to emit aria-label fallback instead when label is absent.
Spec example showed aria-labelledby on the listbox content element; updated to reflect the safer contract (aria-label when no label present, aria-labelledby only when label exists).

workflow: GitHub Actions job-step API can return stale in_progress status for a failed job; cross-referencing with check-run annotations gives the real failure reason.
CI failures with 'No space left on device' in runner diagnostics are infrastructure failures, not code failures — rerun with `gh run rerun <id> --failed`.

The pattern for Codex review loop: trigger with `@codex review` comment, poll reactions (eyes=reviewing, rocket=approved) and unresolved thread count via GraphQL, address threads in batches, re-trigger after each push.
Reply-and-resolve threads in one script using GitHub REST (reply) + GraphQL mutation (resolve) to keep the review loop clean.
cargo fmt --check after applying patches catches expression-level formatting issues that apply_patch doesn't auto-format.

friction: GitHub job-step API returned stale 'in_progress' status for a job that had actually failed, requiring fallback to check-run annotations API to get the real error.
GitHub logs endpoint returned 404 for the failed job, blocking direct log inspection.
The CI failure was a runner infrastructure issue (no disk space) unrelated to code, causing the entire dependent job chain (Feature Flag Check) to fail and requiring a rerun.
Multiple sleep/poll cycles needed because GitHub has no push notification for Codex review completion — required ~10 minutes of 2-minute polling intervals per review pass.
cargo insta accept had path/flag inconsistencies requiring two attempts to accept the correct snapshot.
Codex review triggered three separate passes (second pass, third pass with CI failure, fourth pass with new findings), each requiring the full reply-resolve-push-retrigger cycle.
open_items: The snapshot-reviewed label needs to be re-applied after the latest push (the transcript ends at the commit, before the push and label steps).
A fifth Codex review pass may be triggered on the new commit; the review loop is not yet complete.
The broader CI run triggered by the rerun was still in progress at commit time — final green status not confirmed in transcript.
